exemplar.title: How does the temperature of a resistor affect the current in a circuit?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the temperature of a resistor affect the current in a circuit?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are both clear and precise, with correct headings, units and significant figures.
Gradient calculations for different resistors are shown correctly with proper units.
Key equations (e.g. R(T)=Râ(1+Î±ÎT)) are presented clearly to support analysis.
weaknesses: Uncertainties are calculated once but not applied to every data point or shown on graphs.
Statistical treatment (repeat trials, error bars) is missing, reducing completeness of processing.
Unit notation is inconsistent in places (e.g. âmA / Â°Câ vs âmAÂ·Â°Câ»Â¹â).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication of data recording and processing is both clear and precise (5â6 band), but consideration of uncertainties and data processing each show significant omissions, placing performance at the top of the 3â4 band. A score of 4 is therefore appropriate.

strengths: Research question is clearly situated in a specific, discipline-relevant context linking resistor behavior to practical applications.
Apparatus list is thorough and includes uncertainties, supporting reproducibility.
Step-by-step procedure (voltmeter setting, submersion depth, equilibration time, temperature increments) allows replication with minimal ambiguity.
weaknesses: Procedure omits details on stirring and repeat trials, which could affect reliability.
Methodological considerations are described but not fully explained in terms of ensuring ârelevant and sufficientâ data.
Background section refers to multiple resistor types without justifying their selection or focusing the scope.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands meet the 5â6 band (focused question in specific context; highly reproducible procedure) and one strand sits in the 3â4 band (methodological considerations described rather than fully explained). Overall performance merits a 5 out of 6.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question and is consistent with observed trends.
References to scientific literature (Callister, Halliday, IEEE) situate findings within an accepted context.
weaknesses: Lacks deeper quantitative justification (e.g. calculation of temperature coefficients) to fully support conclusions.
Does not discuss variability between resistor materials or nuances in Î± values.
Comparison to scientific context is present but remains largely qualitative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is described and aligned with analysis, meriting placement in the 3â4 band, but it lacks the full quantitative justification and depth needed for a 5â6. The comparison to scientific context is relevant but cursory, so a score of 3 is justified.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (temperature range, heating stability, resistance fluctuations) are identified.
Realistic improvements (extend temperature range, more resistor types, better heat source) are stated.
weaknesses: The relative impact of each limitation on the validity of results is not analyzed.
Suggested improvements are not explained in terms of how they address identified weaknesses or reduce uncertainties.
Quantitative discussion of how improvements would affect data precision is absent.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Specific weaknesses are described (placing this strand in the 3â4 band), but suggested improvements are merely stated without explanation (1â2 band). Balancing these, the overall evaluation meets the lower half of the 3â4 band, earning a 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student formulates a focused research question and embeds it in a clear, discipline-specific context (reliability of everyday and industrial circuits, temperature-coefficient discussion, choice of carbon/metal resistors).  The motivation and relevance are explicitly connected to practical applications, satisfying the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate context.â Minor stylistic lapses do not detract from the clarity, hence a mark near the lower end of the 5-6 band is appropriate.

Instrument uncertainties are stated and a sample propagation is shown, yet uncertainties are neither applied to every data point nor represented on graphs.  Consequently, consideration is evident but contains significant omissions, fitting the middle of the 3-4 band.

Relevant methodological considerations (instrument precision, control of voltage, immersion medium, safety) are described, but they are not fully explained in terms of how they ensure collection of ârelevant and sufficientâ data (e.g., no justification of single-trial design or choice of only five temperature points).  Description rather than explanation places the work high in the 3-4 band.

Specific weaknesses (restricted temperature range, unstable heating, resistance fluctuation) are identified and briefly described, but their relative impact on validity is not analysed in depth.  This meets the descriptor for 3-4 marks, with quality closer to the lower end.

The apparatus list is complete, and the step-by-step procedure (including voltmeter setting, submersion depth, equilibration time and temperature increments) allows another investigator to reproduce the data collection with only trivial interpretation.  Although stirring and repeat trials are not specified, these omissions are minor; overall reproducibility is still high, warranting placement in the 5-6 band (lower half).

Processing (conversion of units, calculation of gradients, percentage change and one propagated uncertainty) is mostly accurate and internally consistent.  However, the lack of statistical treatment or repeat trials constitutes notable omissions, so the upper mark in the 3-4 band is appropriate.

Improvements (extend temperature range, add more resistor types, use more precise heat source) are realistic but merely stated; they are neither described nor explained in terms of how they address the weaknesses.  This corresponds to the 1-2 band, so a mark of 2 is assigned.

Data tables include correct headings, units and significant figures, and graphs are properly labelled with best-fit lines.  The presentation is simultaneously clear (easy to follow) and precise (values reported to consistent precision), matching the 5-6 descriptor; small layout issues are negligible, so a 5 is awarded.

The student cites textbook data on temperature coefficients and mentions IEEE findings, providing some relevant comparison to accepted scientific context.  The discussion is cursory and largely qualitative; justification is limited, aligning with the mid-band (3) of âsome relevant comparison.â

The conclusion directly answers the research question and is supported by quantitative trends (gradients, linear graphs).  While fully consistent with the analysis, it lacks deeper quantitative justification (e.g., calculations of Î±) that would elevate it to the 5-6 band.  Hence a strong 3-4 band placement is justified.

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively situates the research within realâworld engineering contexts, linking resistor behavior to applications in aerospace and electronics, which strengthens the studyâs relevance.
The controlled variables section ends abruptly and omits details (e.g., stirring, repeat trials). Clarify all controls to ensure reproducibility.
Consider refining the research question to specify resistor materials or types (e.g., carbon vs. metal) for sharper focus and clearer scope.
The background section refers to âmultiple u resistorsâ and does not justify inclusion of high-precision types. Explain the rationale for each resistor category explicitly.
The equation $$R(T)=R_0\bigl(1+\alpha(T-T_0)\bigr)$$ is presented clearly with proper notation, aiding comprehension of temperature dependence.
It would be helpful to define the sign of \(\alpha\) in context (e.g., negative for carbon). Clarify the physical meaning and sign convention for each resistor type.
The apparatus list is thorough and includes instrument uncertainties, enabling reproducibility and highlighting attention to precision.
The procedure omits information on repeat trials and stirring for uniform temperature. Specify trial counts and mixing to improve data reliability.
Include a step to verify equilibrium by recording temperature at multiple time points; this will confirm that the resistor truly reached each set temperature.
The results table comprehensively lists all temperatures and currents, facilitating clear comparison across resistor types.
No uncertainty values are shown in the table or on the subsequent graphs; their omission reduces the precision of data presentation.
To reflect measurement precision, add error bars or uncertainty ranges on each graph corresponding to the Â±0.1âmA and Â±0.1âÂ°C instrument uncertainties.
The gradient calculation for the 150âÎ© resistor is clearly shown with correct substitution and units, enhancing data processing quality.
The analysis notes a âhuge influenceâ of temperature but stops short of quantifying the temperature coefficient Î±; include this calculation for deeper theoretical linkage.
Convert the gradient result into a temperature coefficient \(\alpha\) using \(\alpha=\frac{1}{R_0}\frac{\Delta R}{\Delta T}\) to tie the slope directly to material properties.
The gradient calculation for the 330âÎ© resistor is presented consistently, demonstrating clear and precise data processing.
Repeated formula presentation interrupts flow; reference the earlier derivation of \(\text{gradient}=\frac{y_2-y_1}{x_2-x_1}\) instead of restating it in full.
The gradient unit is shown as âmA / {}^{\circ}Câ in one calculation; standardize to âmAÂ·Â°Câ»Â¹â for consistency and correct notation.
Ensure all unit notations appear uniformly (e.g., âmAÂ·Â°Câ»Â¹â) and explain any deviations between resistor types.
The conversion of 65.9âmA to 0.0659âA demonstrates correct handling of units in sample calculations, improving clarity.
The Ohmâs law verification yields 9.885âV but attribution to âexperimental limitationsâ is vague. Quantify contributions of each uncertainty.
Propagate the calculated ÎV (â0.0673âV) into the sample calculation discussion to explain the 0.115âV discrepancy explicitly.
The propagated uncertainty of â0.14% for current is calculated accurately and demonstrates sound uncertainty analysis.
The propagation formula for ÎV is stated but lacks numerical substitution and final value; include full calculation steps for transparency.
Complete the numeric propagation of ÎV here and compare against the earlier Â±0.00673âV to synthesize your uncertainty analysis.
The conclusion succinctly restates the hypothesis and is aligned with the observed linear trends, offering clear relevance to the research question.
The conclusion claims linear behavior for all resistors but does not discuss variability in temperature coefficient between materials; consider adding this nuance.
Comparisons to Callister (2014), Halliday et al. (2013) and IEEE proceedings enrich the discussion by situating results within accepted scientific literature.
Weaknesses are listed but their relative impact on data validity is not analysed; explain how each limitation affects specific results.
Explain quantitatively how extending temperature range and improving heat source stability would reduce uncertainty in slope and coefficient estimates.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
